layout_description: 4x8 block interior (8x16 tiles). Standard house tileset.
North wall (y=0): Bookshelves at x=0-1; Blackboard at x=3-4 (bg_events).
Earl stands near the north at x=4, y=2 (teacher's position, facing south/down).
Youngster1 at x=2, y=5 (taking notes, facing north/up).
Two Game Boy Kids at x=3-4, y=11 (link trade scene, facing each other).
Youngster2 at x=4, y=7 (discussing held items, facing north/up).
Pokedex (notebook) object at x=2, y=4 (readable, contains catching tutorial).
tile_notes: y=0: Bookshelves at x=0-1 (BG events), Blackboard at x=3-4 (BG events).
y=2: Earl (SPRITE_FISHER) at x=4, facing down.
y=4-5: Youngster1 at x=2, y=5 facing up. Notebook (SPRITE_POKEDEX) at x=2, y=4.
y=7: Youngster2 at x=4, y=7 facing up.
y=11: GameboyKid1 at x=3 facing down; GameboyKid2 at x=4 facing left (link trade pair).
y=15: Exit mat (2 tiles wide, x=3-4).

npcs.name: Earl
npcs.dialogue_summary: Earl introduces himself and offers to teach. Interactive branching dialogue: asks if you want to know how to win (party order tip), then how to raise Pokemon well (EXP share tip). Closing: 'Oh! Smart student you are! Nothing more do I teach!' Spins in place before speaking.

dialogue_tree.intro: EARL, I am! Wonderful are Pokemon, yes! Teach you I will to be a better trainer! What you want to know? Want to be a winner is you?
dialogue_tree.branch_yes_winner: Good! Teach you, I will! In battle, Pokemon top on list jump out first! Change order in list, make battle easy, maybe! More from me you want to hear?
dialogue_tree.branch_yes_raise: Fine! Teach you, I will! If Pokemon come out in battle even briefly, some EXP Points it gets. At top of list put weak Pokemon. Switch in battle quick! This way, weak Pokemon strong become!
dialogue_tree.branch_no_more: Oh! Smart student you are! Nothing more do I teach! Good to Pokemon you must be!
dialogue_tree.branch_no_teach_more: So, want to know how to raise Pokemon well?

npcs.notes: Event flag EVENT_EARLS_ACADEMY_EARL: Earl is only present inside after being escorted from outdoors (cleared by VioletCityEarlScript). If flag is set, Earl does not appear inside yet.

npcs.name: Youngster (Note-taker)
npcs.dialogue_summary: Taking notes of the teacher's lecture. Should copy the stuff on the blackboard too.

npcs.name: Game Boy Kid 1
npcs.dialogue_summary: Traded his best Pokemon to the guy beside him (link trade demo). After dialogue, faces down.

npcs.name: Game Boy Kid 2
npcs.dialogue_summary: Received a Pokemon that is holding something (demonstrating held items). After dialogue, faces down.

npcs.name: Youngster (Held Items)
npcs.dialogue_summary: A Pokemon holding a BERRY will heal itself in battle. Many other items can be held. It sure is tough taking notes...

interactable_objects.description: Interactive blackboard with 2x3 menu listing status conditions. Player selects a topic to read full explanation.

menu_options.label: PSN (Poison)
menu_options.text: If poisoned, a Pokemon steadily loses HP. Poison lingers after the battle, and HP is lost as you walk. To cure it, use an ANTIDOTE.

menu_options.label: PAR (Paralysis)
menu_options.text: Paralysis reduces speed and may prevent movement. It remains after battle, so use a PARLYZ HEAL.

menu_options.label: SLP (Sleep)
menu_options.text: If asleep, your Pokemon can't make a move. A sleeping Pokemon doesn't wake up after battle. Wake it up with an AWAKENING.

menu_options.label: BRN (Burn)
menu_options.text: A burn steadily consumes HP. It also reduces attack power. A burn lingers after battle. Use a BURN HEAL as the cure.

menu_options.label: FRZ (Freeze)
menu_options.text: If your Pokemon is frozen, it can't do a thing. It remains frozen after battle. Thaw it out with an ICE HEAL.

interactable_objects.description: Bookshelf on left side of north wall. Uses standard DifficultBookshelfScript (too difficult to read).
Bookshelf on right side of north wall (left column pair). Uses standard DifficultBookshelfScript.
A student's notebook on a desk. Multi-page tutorial on catching Pokemon. Interactive yes/no prompts to continue reading.
pages: Catch Pokemon using Poke Balls. Up to six can be in your party. Keep reading?
Before throwing a Poke Ball, weaken the target first. A poisoned or burned Pokemon is easier to catch. Keep reading?
Some moves may cause confusion. Confusion may make a Pokemon attack itself. Leaving battle clears up any confusion. Keep reading?

People who catch and use Pokemon in battle are Pokemon trainers. They are expected to visit Pokemon Gyms and defeat other trainers. The next page is... Blank! Boy: E-he-he... I haven't written anymore...

events.description: Earl appears inside the Academy after VioletCityEarlScript (outdoors) clears EVENT_EARLS_ACADEMY_EARL. Before this, Earl is absent from the interior. The flag works inversely: the object_event for Earl inside uses EVENT_EARLS_ACADEMY_EARL, and it is cleared (not set) when Earl enters — meaning Earl is NOT visible until the outdoor script runs.

purpose_and_design.purpose: Tutorial building for new players. Teaches: (1) battle party order, (2) EXP sharing via switching, (3) all five status conditions via blackboard, (4) catching mechanics and held items via notebook and student NPCs.
design_notes: Earl uses broken English throughout ('EARL, I am!', 'Wonderful are Pokemon') for characterization.
The link trade scene between the two Game Boy Kids demonstrates in-game trading and held items simultaneously.
The blackboard is a full interactive menu — unique among early-game buildings.
The notebook (SPRITE_POKEDEX object) is a readable multi-page document, not a character NPC.

AcademyStickerMachine is present in the map script but unreferenced by any object_event — it exists in code only (db '$': AcademyStickerMachineText — sticker machine that prints data). Not currently accessible in the shipped game.
Both bookshelf objects use DifficultBookshelfScript (generic 'too hard to read' response).
Exit warps return to Violet City warp_id 3 (warp_event 30, 17 in VioletCity.asm).

notes: .blk file size: 32 bytes = 4 blocks wide x 8 blocks tall = 8x16 tiles (confirmed). Map has no connections ($00 flags in attributes.asm). Earl's object_event uses EVENT_EARLS_ACADEMY_EARL flag — he only appears inside after the outdoor escort sequence completes.
